engineGuide.h2: BMW 2 Series Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the BMW 2 Series across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: N47 Series - 2.0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Rear timing chain design caused widespread failures. Defined early F22 replacement demand despite strong torque and economy figures.
commonFailures: Rear timing chain stretch or snap, frequently at 60k-80k miles.
DPF differential pressure sensor faults triggering regeneration loops.
Inlet manifold flap motor failure causing uneven air distribution.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N47D20 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: B47 Series - 2.0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Front timing drive replaced the problematic rear setup. Delivered Euro 6 efficiency and improved long-term reliability for F22 and F45 models.
commonFailures: EGR cooler coolant leaks on pre-2019 production batches.
Turbo actuator sticking due to carbon buildup.
AdBlue injector crystallisation blocking supply lines.
entries.cta: Get quotes for B47D20 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: B37 Series - 1.5 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level three-cylinder diesel focused on low running costs and strong urban economy across Active Tourer and Gran Tourer variants.
commonFailures: Injector seal degradation causing compression loss and exhaust smell.
EGR valve carbon buildup from repeated short-trip use.
Glow plug module faults affecting cold-start performance.
entries.cta: Get quotes for B37C15 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: B47 Twin-Turbo Series - 2.0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Twin-turbo B47 derivative used in higher-output 225d applications, delivering stronger mid-range torque and greater thermal load than single-turbo units.
commonFailures: Turbo actuator wear causing boost-control faults.
Charge-air hose pressure leaks under repeated high load.
EGR cooler cracking on earlier production engines.
entries.cta: Get quotes for B47D20T1 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: N13 Series - 1.6 Litre Petrol
entries.history: Early 1.6L turbo for entry F22 models. Prone to timing chain and HPFP issues but delivered strong low-end torque for the compact segment.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattling at startup.
High-pressure fuel pump failure triggering hard starts and limp mode.
Turbocharger wastegate actuator rod wear causing boost fluctuation.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N13B16 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: B38 Series - 1.5 Litre Petrol
entries.history: Compact three-cylinder turbo introduced BMW's modular architecture and prioritised urban efficiency across F45 and newer petrol variants.
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves due to direct injection design.
Water pump and thermostat housing cracks leading to overheating.
Turbocharger oil feed line leaks at higher mileage.
entries.cta: Get quotes for B38B15 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: N20 Series - 2.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: Early direct-injection four-cylinder used before BMW fully transitioned to the B48. It is strongly associated with timing chain guide failures on pre-2017 cars.
commonFailures: Plastic timing chain guide collapse leading to chain slack.
Oil pump drive chain wear causing lubrication risk.
Valve cover gasket leaks and crankcase ventilation faults.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N20B20 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: B48 Series - 2.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: TwinPower turbo unit replacing N20 and N13. Improved thermal management and a stronger long-term reputation across F22, F45, G42 and U06 models.
commonFailures: Plastic coolant pipe fractures near the thermostat housing.
Timing chain guide wear on early F22 and F23 cars.
Turbo wastegate rattling and actuator rod wear.
entries.cta: Get quotes for B48B20 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: N55 Series - 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: Single twin-scroll turbo inline-six powering early M235i models. Delivers strong performance but needs cooling-system diligence at higher mileage.
commonFailures: Electric water pump and thermostat failure around 60k-80k miles.
Turbo wastegate actuator rod wear causing boost fluctuation.
Oil filter housing gasket leaks leading to coolant contamination.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N55B30 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: B58 Series - 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: Closed-deck inline-six replacing N55 in the M240i, with strong thermal management and excellent durability in later performance variants.
commonFailures: Plastic coolant expansion tank leaks under thermal cycling.
Oil filter housing gasket degradation around 50k miles.
High-pressure fuel injector seal wear causing fuel-trim errors.
entries.cta: Get quotes for B58B30 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

entries.title: S55 Series - 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: Motorsport-derived twin-turbo inline-six for the M2 Competition, built for track-focused performance with reinforced internals and high thermal tolerance.
commonFailures: Crank hub slippage on early production units, rare but severe.
Turbocharger oil feed line leaks under sustained high-load driving.
Clutch and flywheel wear accelerated by repeated track use.
entries.cta: Get quotes for S55B30 BMW 2 Series engine replacement

Enter your registration number above - we'll instantly identify the exact unit, its technical specifications, and live replacement prices from UK BMW 2 Series engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.

engineSizes.intro: Every engine displacement officially fitted to the BMW 2 Series is detailed below, complete with compatible engine codes, generation years, and verified failure notes to ensure precise replacement matching.

items.title: BMW 2 Series 1.5L Diesel Engines

compatibleModels: F45/F46 Active Tourer and Gran Tourer
commonFailurePoints: Injector seal degradation causing compression leakage.
EGR valve carbon buildup under repeated short-trip cycles.

items.title: BMW 2 Series 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The backbone of the diesel range across F and G chassis generations, covering N47, B47 and twin-turbo 225d applications with strong torque and long-distance refinement.
commonFailurePoints: N47 timing chain tensioner wear and chain stretch.
B47 EGR cooler cracking under sustained thermal load.

items.title: BMW 2 Series 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Entry-level three-cylinder petrol units delivering strong efficiency and modern BMW turbo response across coupe, Active Tourer and Gran Tourer applications.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain guide wear at higher mileage.

items.title: BMW 2 Series 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Mainstream petrol four-cylinders evolving from the N20 to the more robust B48 architecture, balancing mid-range torque with stronger long-term thermal resilience.

B58 thermostat housing and coolant seal degradation.
S55 track-related oil feed and driveline stress issues.

fuelTypes.h2: BMW 2 Series Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your BMW 2 Series engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early N47D20 diesel units to the latest G42 B58 MHEV variants.

items.title: Diesel Engines
items.description: Dominating the majority of UK replacement enquiries, these efficient triple and inline-four diesels deliver strong torque and motorway economy across F22, F45, F46 and G42 chassis.
items.descriptor: Best for owners prioritising fuel economy, long-distance use and lower running costs.
foundIn: F45 Active Tourer & F46 Gran Tourer (2014-2021)
knownFor: Strong low-end torque and efficient motorway cruising.
Timing chain risk on N47 legacy units.
EGR cooler cracking on B47 engines.
Higher replacement demand after repeated short-trip DPF stress.
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW 2 Series diesel replacement engines

items.title: Petrol Engines
items.description: From efficient entry-level turbo units to full M Performance straight-sixes, the petrol range covers the widest spread of power outputs in the BMW 2 Series lineup.
items.descriptor: Best for mixed driving, smoother refinement and higher-output coupe and performance models.
foundIn: F45 Active Tourer & F46 Gran Tourer
knownFor: Broad performance spread from 136 hp daily drivers to 444 hp M variants.
Timing chain guide issues on early N20 and N13 applications.
Coolant-system weaknesses on B48 petrol engines.
Higher replacement cost on six-cylinder M Performance and M models.
items.cta: Compare BMW 2 Series petrol engine prices

items.title: Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: Later G42 and U06 variants combine B48 or B58 petrol engines with 48V support systems, adding new calibration, charging and control-module considerations during replacement.
items.descriptor: Best for newer-generation owners needing exact ECU, charging and 48V compatibility.
importantNotes: 48V belt-starter generator integration needs specialist coding during engine installation.
DC-DC converter health and charging stability should be checked before any swap.
Hybrid support systems increase fitment complexity versus earlier petrol-only variants.
Use BMW specialists familiar with G42 and U06 electronics to avoid post-fit warning faults.
items.cta: Enquire about BMW 2 Series mild hybrid engine availability

fuelTypes.closing: Not sure which fuel type your BMW 2 Series has? Enter your registration number above to instantly identify your engine code, fuel system and compatible replacement options - all backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK BMW 2 Series specialists.

engineYears.h2: BMW 2 Series Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section maps every major engine change across the BMW 2 Series production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their model year engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Launch-year F22, F23 and F45 cars introduced N47 diesel, N20 petrol and N55 M Performance power.
years.description: BMW's inaugural 2 Series model year introduced rear-wheel-drive coupe and convertible models alongside Active Tourer variants, establishing the N47 diesel and N20/N55 petrol line-up.
keyChanges: F22 Coupe and F23 Convertible debuted alongside F45 Active Tourer and early F46 planning.
N47D20 diesel replaced older M47 architecture with improved emissions compliance.
N55B30 launched as the M235i flagship powertrain.
Early N20B20 petrol engines carried over into mainstream coupe variants.
knownFor: Strong initial UK sales volume driving long-term replacement demand.
Rear-wheel-drive coupe launch appeal.
Early timing chain and fuel-system risks on diesel and petrol legacy units.
years.ticker: Launch-year demand remains strong for early coupe and convertible replacements.
years.cta: Compare 2014 BMW 2 Series engine replacement quotes

years.preview: Major diesel transition year as B47 replaced N47 across core models.
years.description: Mid-cycle engineering revisions introduced the B47D20 engine family, phasing out the problematic N47 and integrating revised EGR cooling architectures.
keyChanges: B47D20 replaced N47D20 across 218d and 220d variants.
Revised timing chain tensioner and guide materials improved diesel reliability.
F45 Active Tourer adopted B47 integration for transverse layout.
Euro 6 emissions compliance standardised across the diesel range.
knownFor: Noticeably better diesel reliability versus 2014 N47 cars.
Early B47 EGR cooler concerns beginning to surface.
Broader Active Tourer adoption in the UK market.
years.ticker: 2015 cars sit at the crossover point between higher-risk N47 and later B47 diesel ownership.
years.cta: Compare 2015 BMW 2 Series engine replacement quotes

years.preview: N20 phased out, B48 standardised and M240i arrived with the B58.
years.description: BMW phased out the legacy N20 family in favour of the thermally stronger B48 architecture, while the M240i replaced the M235i with the newer B58 straight-six.
keyChanges: N20B20 production ended for 220i and 228i variants.
B48B20 became the standard 2.0L petrol across core chassis.
M240i officially launched with the B58B30 powertrain.
Transmission calibration updates improved fitment harmony across B48 and B58 cars.
knownFor: Definitive end of N20 timing guide collapse risk on new cars.
B58 performance benchmark established in the 2 Series range.
Higher enthusiast demand for M240i replacements today.
years.ticker: 2016-2017 models blend strong later-engine reliability with high desirability.
years.cta: Compare 2016-2017 BMW 2 Series engine replacement quotes

years.preview: Facelift era with revised EGR hardware and M2 Competition S55 power.
years.description: Mid-generation facelifts brought updated lighting, revised EGR cooling specifications and full M240i integration, while the M2 Competition added the S55 twin-turbo engine.
keyChanges: F22/F23 LCI styling updates and extra chassis bracing were introduced.
B47 EGR cooler design was revised to address coolant-ingestion leaks.
M2 Competition launched with the S55B30 engine.
Euro 6d-TEMP calibration rollout completed across the range.
knownFor: Peak F-chassis maturity and long-term reliability.
Updated diesel cooling hardware reducing EGR-related failure frequency.
Higher-performance demand from M240i and M2 Competition owners.
years.ticker: Late-F chassis cars are among the most balanced 2 Series choices for replacement economics.
years.cta: Compare 2018-2019 BMW 2 Series engine replacement quotes

years.preview: Final major F-chassis production year before the G42 and U06 shift.
years.description: The last model year for the main F22, F23, F45 and F46 platforms featured fully matured B47, B48 and B58 architectures before the move to the newer generation.
keyChanges: Final ECU calibration updates were rolled out across mature B47 and B48 applications.
M2 Final Edition arrived with tuned S55 output.
Pre-G42 tooling and supply chain changes began.
ZF 8HP transmission mapping was standardised across more rear-wheel and xDrive models.
knownFor: Highly reliable late-F chassis engineering.
Strong residual values shaping replacement-versus-sell decisions.
Updated B48 coolant routing reducing petrol overheating complaints.
years.ticker: 2020 is often the sweet spot for owners comparing repair spend against residual value.
years.cta: Compare 2020 BMW 2 Series engine replacement quotes

years.preview: G42 and U06 launch brought 48V mild-hybrid integration and new coding requirements.
years.description: A full architectural shift to the G42 Coupe and U06 Active Tourer introduced 48V mild-hybrid systems and updated B48 and B58 powertrains across the latest-generation lineup.
keyChanges: G42 Coupe and U06 Active Tourer replaced the core older platforms.
B48B20 and B58B30 gained 48V belt-starter generator integration.
Active Tourer packaging and weight distribution changed engine-fitment considerations.
Advanced driver-assistance integration increased ECU mapping complexity during swaps.
knownFor: First mild-hybrid integration in the 2 Series lineup.
Greater need for specialist coding and calibration after engine replacement.
Higher component integration between engine and electrical systems.
years.ticker: Newer 48V cars require tighter fitment control and more specialist post-install coding.
years.cta: Compare 2021-2022 BMW 2 Series engine replacement quotes

years.preview: Current-generation refinements focus on software stability, hybrid efficiency and higher-output B58 maturity.
years.description: Continuous calibration updates and minor component revisions across the current G42 and U06 range focus on software stability, emissions compliance and hybrid-system efficiency.
keyChanges: B48 ECU mapping was revised for stronger real-world fuel economy.
B58 validation was updated for higher boost tolerance on M240i models.
Newer iDrive software became more tightly integrated with engine management.
ULEZ compliance remained aligned across petrol and updated diesel variants.
knownFor: High baseline reliability in the latest generation.
Software-related limp modes and warning resets rather than major hardware redesigns.
Greater integration between hybrid support systems and core engine controls.
years.ticker: Latest-generation replacements are often less about block failure and more about exact-code, exact-software matching.
years.cta: Compare 2023-2025 BMW 2 Series engine replacement quotes

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your BMW 2 Series was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK BMW 2 Series specialists.

faq.h2: BMW 2 Series Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Answers to the most common BMW 2 Series replacement-engine questions, based on current UK specialist pricing and known failure patterns across diesel, petrol and M Performance models.

items.question: How much does a BMW 2 Series engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt BMW 2 Series engine typically costs £1,650-£4,500 supply-only depending on engine code and variant. Main dealer replacements often exceed £5,000, while specialist rebuilt or reconditioned units usually offer the best value.
keyPoints: B47D20 diesel rebuilds average £1,950-£3,600 supply-only across UK specialists.
N47 legacy units cost less but should include upgraded timing components.
Petrol B48B20 replacements usually land between £1,800 and £3,800.
Supply and fit typically adds £650-£950 plus any extra coding or delivery costs.
items.cta: Compare live BMW 2 Series engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: Is the N47 diesel engine in the BMW 2 Series reliable?
items.answer: The N47D20 in pre-2015 BMW 2 Series models is known for timing chain tensioner and guide failure, often leading to severe internal damage between 60k and 100k miles. Later B47-powered diesel variants are significantly stronger long-term options.
keyPoints: Cold-start rattling is the classic warning sign on N47 engines.
Upgraded timing kits can prevent failure if fitted early enough.
Rebuilt N47 units should include revised timing hardware.
B47D20 engines from 2015 onward improved chain geometry and durability.
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt N47D20 BMW 2 Series engines with upgraded timing components
items.warning: Ignoring cold-start rattling on N47 engines risks complete engine seizure without warning.

items.question: What causes the B47 EGR cooler to fail on BMW 2 Series diesels?
items.answer: BMW 2 Series B47D20 engines can suffer EGR cooler cracking because the original aluminium housings are repeatedly stressed by heat cycles. Once coolant reaches the intake or combustion path, replacement costs rise sharply and full rebuilds are often the safer route.
keyPoints: White smoke, coolant loss and misfires are common early symptoms.
Coolant ingress can lead to hydrolock and bearing washout.
Rebuilt B47 replacements should include the revised EGR cooler design.
DPF contamination often adds cost if the leak is left unresolved.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt B47D20 BMW 2 Series engines with upgraded EGR coolers included
items.warning: Continued driving with EGR coolant leaks risks hydrolock and irreversible piston damage.

items.question: Should I buy a used BMW 2 Series with the N20 petrol engine?
items.answer: Only if the timing chain guide issue has already been addressed or you budget for it immediately. Pre-2017 N20-powered BMW 2 Series cars are affordable to buy, but guide failure can turn them into expensive engine-replacement candidates very quickly.
keyPoints: High-pitched whining or rattling from the timing cover is a major warning sign.
Rebuilt N20 engines should include reinforced guides and revised tensioners.
Post-2017 B48 cars generally offer better long-term peace of mind.
A compression test and service-history review are essential before purchase.
items.cta: Find rebuilt N20B20 BMW 2 Series engines with upgraded timing components and warranty
items.warning: Driving an N20 with guide noise risks catastrophic valve and piston damage without warning.

items.question: How long do BMW 2 Series engines typically last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: Well-maintained BMW 2 Series engines can exceed 150,000 miles, but lifespan depends heavily on engine code. Later B47 and B48 units often reach 120k-180k miles, while N47 and N20 failures are more tied to design weaknesses than service discipline alone.
keyPoints: Full service history helps B47 and B48 engines live much longer.
N47 and N20 timing failures can still happen despite good maintenance.
Short-trip diesel use accelerates DPF and EGR issues.
Rebuilt engines with upgraded weak-point components often outlast original factory spec.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt BMW 2 Series engines with upgraded components for extended reliability

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW 2 Series engine or buying another car?
items.answer: In many cases, yes. A rebuilt BMW 2 Series engine usually costs far less than replacing the whole vehicle, and it lets you keep a chassis and history you already know rather than gambling on another used car.
keyPoints: Typical engine replacement spend is lower than buying a comparable used replacement car.
Specialist rebuilt units address factory weak points directly.
Supply-and-fit packages reduce installation and coding risk.
You avoid extra depreciation and finance costs tied to changing vehicles.
items.cta: Get tailored BMW 2 Series engine replacement quotes to compare against used car prices

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned, rebuilt and used BMW 2 Series engines?
items.answer: Used engines are budget donor units, reconditioned engines restore worn components to a safe standard, and rebuilt engines undergo deeper teardown and upgraded internal work. For known BMW 2 Series weak points, rebuilt units are usually the safest long-term choice.
keyPoints: Used engines are cheapest but carry the highest unknown internal wear risk.
Reconditioned units restore tolerances but may not always address the original weak design.
Rebuilt engines should include upgrades for N47 timing, B47 EGR and N20 guide issues.
Remanufactured units are often the most factory-spec option but come at a higher price.
items.cta: Compare reconditioned versus rebuilt BMW 2 Series engine options with warranty details

items.question: Will a replacement BMW 2 Series engine pass MoT and ULEZ requirements?
items.answer: Yes, provided the replacement is correctly specified, installed and calibrated. Rebuilt BMW 2 Series engines from specialist suppliers should be checked for DPF health, ECU readiness and emissions-system integrity before the car returns to the road.
keyPoints: Diesel replacements should include DPF and regeneration validation.
Petrol B48 and B58 units benefit from revised cooling hardware to prevent emissions-related overheating issues.
ECU adaptation is critical so readiness monitors complete correctly for MoT testing.
ULEZ status depends on the vehicle's registration and emissions category, not just the engine swap.
items.cta: Find MoT-compliant rebuilt BMW 2 Series engines with ULEZ validation documentation included

items.question: Can I fit a B48 engine from a 3 Series into my BMW 2 Series?
items.answer: Not as a simple direct swap. Even where the core block is related, BMW 2 Series installations often need chassis-specific ECU coding, ancillaries, bracket changes and drivetrain adaptation to work properly and keep warranty cover intact.
keyPoints: F22 Coupe and F30 3 Series models share architecture but not identical software or ancillaries.
xDrive and transmission calibration must match the receiving chassis.
Coolant routing and mounting positions vary between platforms.
Specialist rebuilders can pre-adapt a replacement engine to your exact VIN and configuration.
items.cta: Enquire about chassis-specific BMW 2 Series engine replacements with pre-adapted ECU coding
items.warning: Incorrect ECU adaptation during engine swaps can trigger immobiliser lockout requiring dealer reset.

items.question: How do I know if my BMW 2 Series needs a full engine replacement?
items.answer: Persistent knocking, coolant and oil mixing, heavy smoke, severe compression loss or confirmed internal scoring all point toward replacement rather than a light repair. Once internal damage is verified, a rebuilt engine is often the most economical long-term fix.
keyPoints: Cold-start rattling on N47 or N20 engines suggests timing-system failure.
Coolant in oil or white smoke can indicate B47 or B48 cooling-system-related internal damage.
Low compression across cylinders usually confirms serious internal wear.
Metal in the sump or oil filter is a strong sign that teardown or replacement is unavoidable.
items.cta: Get a specialist diagnostic assessment for your BMW 2 Series engine before deciding
items.warning: Continuing to drive with knocking or smoke risks complete engine seizure and more expensive secondary damage.
